items.name: ARPA-E HORNIG
items.provides: Cost-shared R&D funding to design, fabricate, test and qualify transuranic (TRU) fuels for Gen IV reactors from recycled/reserve fissile material
items.deadline_label: Concept Paper deadline passed 2026-05-28; Full Application NOFO/deadline not yet published (TBD)
items.fit: Directly matches newcleo's closed fuel cycle / TRU-fuel and Gen IV fast reactor fuel priorities
items.funds: Up to $50M total program; individual awards $500K–$7M
items.note: <cite index="2-8">The U.S. Department of Energy's (DOE) Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy (ARPA-E) today announced up to $50 million in funding to develop transuranic (TRU) fuels for use in advanced reactors using materials recovered from America's reserves of used nuclear fuel and other strategic materials.</cite> <cite index="41-1">Concept papers are due by 5/28/2026; Full application deadline is TBD; Award amounts will range from $500K to $7M.</cite> This NOFO (DE-FOA-0003623) appears to be the formal outgrowth of the earlier 'Advanced Reactor Fuels' teaming-partner list — track together, do not double count.
items.change_note: Status update: full NOFO (DE-FOA-0003623) formally released 4/23/2026; concept-paper phase has now closed (5/28/2026); full-application phase still pending with no published deadline

items.name: ARPA-E Advanced Reactor Fuels
items.provides: Team-formation list ahead of a potential nuclear-fuels NOFO; no funding itself
items.deadline_label: NOFO originally expected by Feb 2026; appears superseded by HORNIG (released 4/23/26)
items.fit: Same TRU/recycled-fuel scope as HORNIG; likely the precursor announcement
items.funds: N/A — teaming list only, pending NOFO
items.note: <cite index="40-9,40-10">A NOFO does not exist at this time; applicants must refer to the NOFO, expected to be issued by February 2026.</cite> <cite index="78-14,78-18">This potential funding opportunity would aim to support the development of transuranic (TRU) fuels that use a largely untapped domestic fissile resource found across U.S. inventories</cite> — language identical to HORNIG's scope, indicating this teaming list fed into the now-released HORNIG NOFO. Treat as the same underlying opportunity as HORNIG going forward; verify on next site check whether ARPA-E retires this separate listing.
items.change_note: Likely superseded/merged into the released HORNIG NOFO (DE-FOA-0003623); no separate NOFO has appeared under this name as of program's original Feb 2026 target

items.name: NEUP
items.fit: Accessible only through a US university partner; fuel, materials and licensing R&D.
items.funds: University-led nuclear energy R&D awards (Nuclear Energy University Program).
items.note: Requires a US university PI. A partnership route, not a direct newcleo award.

items.name: NEUP (FY2026 CINR / CINR Phase II / DECP / Infrastructure Revitalization NOFOs)
items.sponsor: DOE Office of Nuclear Energy / INL (NEUP)
items.provides: Competitive research funding to US universities (industry/labs only as subrecipients)
items.deadline_label: FY2026 round deadlines have now passed: CINR R&D full application 2026-06-09; CINR Phase II full application 2026-05-20; DECP (extended) 2026-03-10
items.fit: Low-medium direct fit for newcleo since prime applicant must be a US university; relevant only as a lab/industry subrecipient pathway
items.funds: Multi-million dollar annual NOFO pool; awards vary by topic
items.note: <cite index="68-1,68-2">Modification 003 for the FY26 CINR NOFO changed the NSUF Full-Application Statement of Work deadline to May 21, 2026; the application deadline for this NOFO is June 9, 2026.</cite> <cite index="70-7,70-8">CINR Phase II full application was due May 20, 2026, at 5:00 p.m. ET.</cite> <cite index="76-9,76-10">The FY 2026 Distinguished Early Career Program application deadline was extended to March 10, 2026.</cite> All FY2026 cycle deadlines have now elapsed as of the July 2026 report date; next cycle (FY2027) not yet posted — check neup.inl.gov in autumn 2026.
items.change_note: FY2026 round NOFOs (CINR, CINR II, DECP) have all closed — deadlines passed between March–June 2026; program remains 'rolling' only in the sense that a new annual cycle is expected later in 2026, but nothing is currently open for FY2026

items.name: NRIC DOME Test Bed Access (RFA)
items.sponsor: DOE / National Reactor Innovation Center (NRIC), Idaho National Laboratory
items.provides: Access to INL's Demonstration of Microreactor Experiments (DOME) test bed for private reactor developers, on a full cost-recovery basis
items.fit: Low-medium for newcleo directly (DOME is currently microreactor-focused, e.g. Radiant's Kaleidos), but relevant as a national-lab access mechanism parallel to Launch Pad for any future fuel-cycle/reactor testing need
items.funds: No cash; applicant bears all costs of test-bed use
items.note: <cite index="32-2,32-3">NRIC also opened a standalone solicitation for private developers to access its Demonstration of Microreactor Experiments (DOME) test bed at Idaho National Laboratory; DOME is currently slated to host Radiant Nuclear's Kaleidos microreactor under its first fueled test campaign, and its RFA runs on the same July 8 deadline but is independent of the Launch Pad process.</cite>
items.change_note: Net-new: separate DOME test-bed RFA opened alongside NRIC Launch Pad, same 2026-07-08 initial deadline

items.name: GAIN NE Vouchers
items.fit: Direct access to national-lab capability for fuel fabrication, lead-cooled materials testing and licensing analysis.
items.funds: Access to DOE national-lab expertise and facilities at no cost; recipient provides 20% cost-share via a CRADA.
items.note: Recipients get lab access, not money. Eligibility ties resulting IP to US manufacturing: confirm newcleo Americas fit.

items.name: Fuel-cycle resource FOAs
items.provides: Site access, used-fuel material, lab expertise
items.fit: Access to DOE sites, used-fuel material and national-lab expertise for fuel-cycle work.
items.funds: DE-FOA-0003611 and DE-FOA-0000001: provide site access, used-fuel material and lab expertise. Applicant carries every dollar.
items.note: Not capital. These are resource-access, applicant self-funds entirely. Confirm current status before acting.

items.name: DOE Center for Used Fuel Research (CUFR)
items.sponsor: DOE Office of Nuclear Energy / Idaho National Laboratory
items.provides: Hosted research center at INL for used/spent nuclear fuel R&D; periodically issues technology-selection calls (e.g., canister monitoring) rather than cash grants
items.deadline_label: No fixed deadline identified; center runs periodic technology calls
items.fit: Directly relevant to newcleo's closed fuel cycle / spent-fuel recycling priority topic
items.funds: No direct cash to applicants confirmed; provides lab access/collaboration and has selected vendor technologies for demonstration
items.note: <cite index="20-6">INL to host Center for Used Fuel Research · January 15, 2026 · The Department of Energy's Office of Nuclear Energy announced the establishment of the Center for Used Fuel Research (CUFR), to be hosted at the Idaho National Laboratory</cite>. Center has already run at least one technology-selection call (automated used-fuel canister monitoring); newcleo should monitor for fuel-recycling-relevant calls given its spent-fuel/MOX focus. Deadline/eligibility details not yet independently verified on an official CUFR page.
items.change_note: Net-new: newly established (Jan 2026) DOE lab center focused on used-fuel R&D, not previously tracked

items.name: NRIC Nuclear Energy Launch Pad (Launch Pad INL / Launch Pad USA)
items.sponsor: DOE / National Reactor Innovation Center (NRIC) at Idaho National Laboratory
items.kind: DOE authorization + site-access pathway (Request for Applications), rolling
items.provides: DOE Atomic Energy Act authorization (bypassing initial NRC licensing), pre-characterized federal land/site access, and technical assistance for reactor, fuel-fabrication, recycling or enrichment facilities; no cash — applicant funds all design, construction, operation and decommissioning
items.deadline_label: Initial-round submission deadline July 8, 2026 (5pm MDT); RFA remains open on a continual rolling basis thereafter, with further selections announced periodically
items.fit: High — covers fuel-fabrication and recycling facilities explicitly, and offers an alternative/complementary DOE authorization route to newcleo's Savannah River fuel-fab and LFR ambitions
items.funds: No direct funding; site parcels, DOE authorization pathway, and technical/regulatory assistance only
items.note: NRIC and DOE are establishing the Launch Pad to promote rapid private-industry deployment of advanced nuclear technologies, building on the Reactor and Fuel Line Pilot Programs. The RFA invites developers of reactors, enrichment facilities, fuel fabrication plants, and reprocessing/recycling operations to apply. Foreign-ownership review (relevant given newcleo Americas' European parent) can extend timelines — applicants have asked NRIC to clarify whether Strategic Partnership Project/OTA foreign-ownership review can begin in parallel with selection.

items.name: SPUR
items.provides: Same as DOE Surplus Plutonium Utilization Program entry — duplicate short-name tracking
items.fit: Direct relevance via Oklo/newcleo Savannah River partnership
items.note: See 'DOE Surplus Plutonium Utilization Program (SPUR)' entry for full detail; <cite index="62-13,62-14">the other four companies selected are Exodys Energy, SHINE Technologies, Standard Nuclear, and Flibe Energy; the negotiations are not yet finalized.</cite>
items.change_note: Same status advance as above — negotiations phase confirmed, not yet a finalized allocation

items.name: CRADA
items.fit: Direct R&D partnership with a national lab (INL, ANL, ORNL, SRNL) on fuel and lead-cooled materials.
items.funds: Collaborative R&D: the lab brings expertise and facilities, the partner funds its own share. No cash to newcleo.
items.note: A mechanism, not a dated call. Can be initiated at any time, including under a GAIN voucher.

items.name: DOE Fuel Line Pilot Program
items.sponsor: U.S. DOE (Office of Nuclear Energy), authorization-based RFA
items.kind: DOE authorization pathway for advanced nuclear fuel fabrication lines (non-NRC)
items.provides: DOE authorization to build/operate a fuel fabrication line outside national laboratories, intended to unlock private funding and fast-track future NRC licensing; no cash
items.deadline_label: No fixed deadline confirmed; DOE has run at least two selection rounds (2025 and mid-2026) and continues to make selections as capacity allows
items.fit: Medium-high — Oklo (newcleo's Savannah River MOX partner) has already used this exact mechanism for fuel-fabrication facilities, illustrating the pathway newcleo Americas could also use for its own facility
items.funds: No direct funding; each company is responsible for all costs associated with construction, operation, and decommissioning of their fuel fabrication facilities, and must manage sourcing of nuclear material feedstock
items.note: Oklo was selected to build and operate three fuel fabrication facilities to support its Aurora and Pluto reactors, and possibly other fast reactors. Not yet confirmed whether a new open call exists for additional entrants beyond the two rounds already run.

items.name: DOE Surplus Plutonium Utilization Program (SPUR)
items.sponsor: DOE Office of Nuclear Energy / NNSA material custodian
items.provides: Allocation of surplus weapons-grade plutonium as feedstock for fuel fabrication; applicant funds and builds/operates the processing facility
items.deadline_label: No open call — 5 firms in advanced negotiations since 2026-05-27; negotiations ongoing
items.fit: Core to newcleo's US entry strategy: Oklo (newcleo's US fuel-fabrication partner) was one of five companies selected for negotiations, with newcleo's Savannah River MOX/plutonium fuel facility as the intended downstream use
items.funds: No cash; material only. Recipient bears all facility design/construction/operation/decommissioning costs
items.note: <cite index="61-2,61-3">Earlier this year, the Department competitively selected Exodys Energy, Flibe Energy, Oklo, SHINE Technologies, and Standard Nuclear for advanced negotiations related to potential allocation of the plutonium materials. Negotiations began on May 27, 2026, and are currently ongoing.</cite> <cite index="61-8">Selected applicants will be responsible for all costs associated with a DOE-authorized recycling and processing facility, including the design, construction, operation, and decommissioning.</cite> <cite index="59-10">In partnership with newcleo, a European developer of advanced nuclear reactors, Oklo would lead the utilization of surplus plutonium, while newcleo would bring relevant fuel experience and pot[ential project capital]</cite>. newcleo's access is indirect (via Oklo's selection), not a direct newcleo award — flag this eligibility nuance for a European-owned applicant.
items.change_note: Status advanced from open solicitation to formal 'advanced negotiations' with 5 named companies (incl. Oklo/newcleo's partner) as of 2026-05-26/27; not yet a signed allocation agreement

items.name: ARPA-E NEWTON
items.fit: Used-fuel transmutation R&D, lab-led and already awarded.
items.funds: Transmutation technology R&D. Awards made in 2025 (Argonne, Fermilab and others).

items.name: ARDP
items.fit: The large demonstration money, effectively committed to the X-energy and TerraPower cohort.
items.funds: Advanced Reactor Demonstration Program cost-share for first-of-a-kind builds.
items.note: Reference line, not a live opportunity for newcleo.
